name: server.R
ui.R
README_DEPLOY.txt
content:
GlobalSoilMacroFauna Dataset Checker
====================================

Folder structure
----------------
global.R
server.R
ui.R
www/gsmf_logo.png
reference_template/GlobalSoilMacrofauna_template_v.2.2.xlsx

Local Shiny test
----------------
shiny::runApp("C:/github_repos/app_dataset_checker")

Export as a static Shinylive site
---------------------------------
shinylive::export(
  appdir = "C:/github_repos/app_dataset_checker",
  destdir = "C:/github_repos/app_dataset_checker_site"
)

Test the exported site locally
------------------------------
httpuv::runStaticServer("C:/github_repos/app_dataset_checker_site")

Required R packages
-------------------
shiny, readxl, visdat, DT, leaflet, tidyverse, shinylive, httpuv

Notes
-----
- The workbook is processed locally in the browser in the Shinylive version.
- The private global GSMF dataset is not bundled in this application.
- A later comparison service can be implemented separately on Shiny Server.

name: add_loading_overlay.R
code_export_app.r
content:
# Export Shinylive

app_dir  <- "C:/github_repos/app_dataset_checker"
site_dir <- "C:/github_repos/globalsoilmacrofauna/globalsoilmacrofauna.github.io/dataset_checker"

# Remove only the previous Shiny app export
if (dir.exists(site_dir)) {
  unlink(site_dir, recursive = TRUE, force = TRUE)
}

shinylive::export(
  appdir = app_dir,
  destdir = site_dir
)

# Ajoute l'écran de chargement
source("add_loading_overlay.R")
add_loading_overlay(site_dir)

# Local test
httpuv::runStaticServer(
  site_dir,
  port = 8000
)

name: global.R
content:
# ==============================================================================
# global.R
# #GlobalSoilMacroFauna - dataset checker
#
# This file:
#   1) loads packages
#   2) points to a bundled copy of the OFFICIAL template (kept in
#      reference_template/) which is used both as a reference for the
#      "Variables explanation" spec (mandatory fields, allowed values, units)
#      and as a fallback for header detection
#   3) defines generic, defensive helper functions used by server.R to read
#      and validate any uploaded workbook, regardless of small structural
#      differences between template versions (number of rows before the
#      header, exact sheet names, etc.)
# ==============================================================================

library(shiny)
library(readxl)   # reading Excel files
library(visdat)    # dataset structure visualization
library(DT)        # interactive tables
library(leaflet)   # lightweight interactive map
library(tidyverse)  # dplyr / tidyr / stringr / tibble / readr / ggplot2

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 0. Configuration
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Bundled copy of the current OFFICIAL template. Used to build the validation
# spec automatically from the "Variables explanation" sheet, so that updating
# this one file is enough to keep the app in sync with future template
# versions (no need to touch the R code).
#
# The exact filename is NOT hardcoded (version numbers / separators change,
# e.g. "v_2_2" vs "v.2.2"): the app just picks the first .xlsx file found in
# reference_template/, preferring one whose name contains
# "globalsoilmacrofauna" if there are several.
REFERENCE_TEMPLATE_DIR <- "reference_template"

find_reference_template <- function(dir = REFERENCE_TEMPLATE_DIR) {
  if (!dir.exists(dir)) return(NA_character_)
  files <- list.files(dir, pattern = "\\.xlsx$", full.names = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)
  if (length(files) == 0) return(NA_character_)

  preferred <- files[grepl("globalsoilmacrofauna", basename(files), ignore.case = TRUE)]
  if (length(preferred) > 0) return(preferred[1])
  files[1]
}

REFERENCE_TEMPLATE_PATH <- find_reference_template()

# Strings that should be treated as missing values when importing datasets.
# (kept case-variants because data providers are not always consistent)
NA_CODES <- c(
  "na", "NA", "Na", "nA", "n.a", "N.A",
  "nd", "ND", "Nd",
  "n/a", "N/A",
  "nr", "NR", "Nr",
  "unknown", "Unknown", "UNKNOWN",
  "-", "--", ""
)

# Column-name prefixes that are intentionally open-ended in the template
# (users can add new taxa / soil properties columns as long as they use
# these prefixes), so they should never be flagged as "unrecognized columns".
OPEN_PREFIXES <- c("^d_", "^fbm_", "^dbm_", "^soil_", "^litter_")

# Additional GSMF columns intentionally added after the official template.
# These columns are accepted by the checker even though they are absent from
# the reference workbook, and are therefore not reported as unrecognized.
ALLOWED_EXTRA_COLUMNS <- c(
  "lu_fao",
  "lu_esa"
)

# Fields that should be treated as free text even when the
# "Variables explanation" sheet assigns an incorrect or overly restrictive
# format. These fields still undergo all other relevant checks, including
# presence, missing-value and within-transect consistency checks.
TEXT_FIELDS <- c(
  "sample_shape",
  "spatial_setting",
  "time_since_former_LU",
  "country",
  "state",
  "locality"
)

# Unit columns contain labels such as "g_cm3", "percentage" or
# "percentage of dry mass". The reference template often shows one preferred
# unit (for example Mg_m3 or g_kg), but this is not a numeric-format rule and
# alternative units may be valid. Therefore all fields ending in "_unit" are
# treated as free text by the generic validator.
UNIT_COLUMN_PATTERN <- "_unit$"

# Sheet-name candidates, tried in order (case-insensitive, regex), for each
# logical sheet we need. Used across template versions 1.x / 2.x.
DATASET_SHEET_PATTERNS  <- c("^dataset$", "^dataset template$", "^1_macrofauna$", "dataset")
METADATA_SHEET_PATTERNS <- c("^metadata to fill$", "^dataset info$", "^0_dataset info$", "metadata", "info")
SPEC_SHEET_PATTERNS     <- c("^variables explanation$", "variable", "explanation")

METADATA_EXCLUDE_FROM_DATASET <- c(
  "metadata to fill", "dataset info", "0_dataset info",
  "variables explanation", "explanations", "authorized values"
)

# Common column-naming variants seen across submitted files (derived from
# the naming issues you fix by hand most often). Applied to an unrecognized
# column name to see if it is a known variant of an official field, so the
# validation report can suggest a rename instead of a plain "unknown column".
COLUMN_NAME_ALIASES <- c(
  "^n_sples$"                                   = "transect_size",
  "^n_sple_trsct$"                               = "transect_size",
  "agroforestery"                                = "agroforestry",
  "^covercrop$"                                  = "cover_crop",
  "detail$"                                      = "details",
  "^elev$"                                       = "altitude",
  "fertiliser"                                   = "fertilizer",
  "^fertilizers_non_chemical$"                   = "fertilizers_organic",
  "^irrigation_detail$"                          = "irrigation_details",
  "^microhabitat$"                               = "monolith_microhabitat",
  "^monoculture_polyculture$|^monoculturepolyculture$" = "monoculture_polyculture",
  "^orchad$"                                     = "orchard",
  "^rightsholder$"                               = "data_provider",
  "^rotation_detail$"                            = "rotation_details",
  "^sample_id$"                                  = "monolith_id",
  "^sple_"                                       = "sample_",
  "^trsct_"                                      = "transect_",
  "^treatment$"                                  = "management",
  "vegatation"                                   = "vegetation"
)

# Legacy taxa columns sometimes use a "_d" / "_fbm" / "_dbm" SUFFIX instead
# of the official "d_" / "fbm_" / "dbm_" PREFIX (older template versions).
TAXA_NAME_FIXES <- c(
  "^([a-z_]+)_d$"   = "d_\\1",
  "^([a-z_]+)_fbm$" = "fbm_\\1",
  "^([a-z_]+)_dbm$" = "dbm_\\1"
)

# Taxon abundance columns are supported in both the current naming convention
# (d_taxon, fbm_taxon, dbm_taxon) and the legacy convention
# (taxon_d, taxon_fbm, taxon_dbm). Legacy names remain valid for reading and
# checking, but a warning recommends conversion to the current convention.
is_current_taxa_column <- function(x) {
  grepl("^(d_|fbm_|dbm_).+", x, ignore.case = TRUE)
}

is_legacy_taxa_column <- function(x) {
  grepl("^.+_(d|fbm|dbm)$", x, ignore.case = TRUE)
}

is_taxa_column <- function(x) {
  is_current_taxa_column(x) | is_legacy_taxa_column(x)
}

current_taxa_column_name <- function(x) {
  out <- as.character(x)
  legacy <- is_legacy_taxa_column(out)
  if (any(legacy)) {
    m <- stringr::str_match(out[legacy], regex("^(.+)_(d|fbm|dbm)$", ignore_case = TRUE))
    out[legacy] <- paste0(tolower(m[, 3]), "_", m[, 2])
  }
  out
}

extract_taxon_name <- function(x) {
  x <- as.character(x)
  out <- rep(NA_character_, length(x))

  current <- is_current_taxa_column(x)
  out[current] <- sub("^(d_|fbm_|dbm_)", "", x[current], ignore.case = TRUE)

  legacy <- !current & is_legacy_taxa_column(x)
  out[legacy] <- sub("_(d|fbm|dbm)$", "", x[legacy], ignore.case = TRUE)

  out
}

# Common soil-property column naming variants seen across submitted files
# (element abbreviations, typos, alternate suffix spellings...). Applied in
# the same way as COLUMN_NAME_ALIASES to suggest a rename for unrecognized
# soil_* columns.
SOIL_NAME_ALIASES <- c(
  "^avaliable_|^available_"                          = "",
  "aluminum|alumnium"                                = "aluminium",
  "^bulk_density$|^bulkdensity$|soil_bd$"            = "soil_bulk_density",
  "basess"                                            = "bases",
  "_content$"                                         = "",
  "mehtod"                                            = "method",
  "phosphorous"                                       = "phosphorus",
  "postassium"                                        = "potassium",
  "pariculate"                                        = "particulate",
  "soiltexture"                                       = "soil_texture",
  "soil_humidity$|soil_water_content$"                = "soil_moisture",
  "ph_water"                                          = "ph_h2o",
  "soil_cic"                                          = "soil_cec",
  "^soil_cec$"                                        = "soil_cation_exchange_capacity",
  "soil_bases_saturation"                             = "soil_base_saturation",
  "^soil_bases$"                                      = "soil_sum_of_bases",
  "soil_oc$|soil_soc$|soil_c_org$|soil_organic_c$|soil_ocarbon$" = "soil_organic_carbon",
  "soil_oc_unit$|soil_soc_unit$|soil_organic_c_unit$"           = "soil_organic_carbon_unit",
  "soil_oc_method$|soil_soc_method$|soil_organic_c_method$"     = "soil_organic_carbon_method",
  "soil_oc_depth$|soil_soc_depth$|soil_organic_c_depth$"        = "soil_organic_carbon_depth",
  "soil_om$|soil_som$|soil_org_mat$"                  = "soil_organic_matter",
  "soil_om_unit$|soil_som_unit$|soil_org_mat_unit$"   = "soil_organic_matter_unit",
  "soil_om_method$|soil_som_method$|soil_org_mat_meth$" = "soil_organic_matter_method",
  "soil_om_depth$|soil_som_depth$|soil_org_mat_depth$" = "soil_organic_matter_depth",
  "soil_n$|soil_total_n$"                             = "soil_total_nitrogen",
  "soil_n_unit$|soil_total_n_unit$"                   = "soil_total_nitrogen_unit",
  "soil_n_method$|soil_total_n_method$"               = "soil_total_nitrogen_method",
  "soil_n_depth$|soil_total_n_depth$"                 = "soil_total_nitrogen_depth",
  "soil_cn$"                                          = "soil_cn_ratio",
  # element abbreviation -> full name (with _unit/_method/_depth variants)
  "soil_al$"     = "soil_aluminium",     "soil_al_unit$"     = "soil_aluminium_unit",     "soil_al_method$"     = "soil_aluminium_method",     "soil_al_depth$"     = "soil_aluminium_depth",
  "soil_ca$"     = "soil_calcium",       "soil_ca_unit$"     = "soil_calcium_unit",       "soil_ca_method$"     = "soil_calcium_method",       "soil_ca_depth$"     = "soil_calcium_depth",
  "soil_fe$"     = "soil_iron",          "soil_fe_unit$"     = "soil_iron_unit",          "soil_fe_method$"     = "soil_iron_method",          "soil_fe_depth$"     = "soil_iron_depth",
  "soil_mn$"     = "soil_manganese",     "soil_mn_unit$"     = "soil_manganese_unit",     "soil_mn_method$"     = "soil_manganese_method",     "soil_mn_depth$"     = "soil_manganese_depth",
  "soil_k$"      = "soil_potassium",     "soil_k_unit$"      = "soil_potassium_unit",     "soil_k_method$"      = "soil_potassium_method",     "soil_k_depth$"      = "soil_potassium_depth",
  "soil_na$"     = "soil_sodium",        "soil_na_unit$"     = "soil_sodium_unit",        "soil_na_method$"     = "soil_sodium_method",        "soil_na_depth$"     = "soil_sodium_depth",
  "soil_p$"      = "soil_phosphorus",    "soil_p_unit$"      = "soil_phosphorus_unit",    "soil_p_method$"      = "soil_phosphorus_method",    "soil_p_depth$"      = "soil_phosphorus_depth",
  "soil_pb$"     = "soil_lead",          "soil_pb_unit$"     = "soil_lead_unit",          "soil_pb_method$"     = "soil_lead_method",          "soil_pb_depth$"     = "soil_lead_depth",
  "soil_zn$"     = "soil_zinc",          "soil_zn_unit$"     = "soil_zinc_unit",          "soil_zn_method$"     = "soil_zinc_method",          "soil_zn_depth$"     = "soil_zinc_depth",
  "soil_cu$"     = "soil_copper",        "soil_cu_unit$"     = "soil_copper_unit",        "soil_cu_method$"     = "soil_copper_method",        "soil_cu_depth$"     = "soil_copper_depth",
  "soil_ni$"     = "soil_nickel",        "soil_ni_unit$"     = "soil_nickel_unit",        "soil_ni_method$"     = "soil_nickel_method",        "soil_ni_depth$"     = "soil_nickel_depth",
  "soil_co$"     = "soil_cobalt",        "soil_co_unit$"     = "soil_cobalt_unit",        "soil_co_method$"     = "soil_cobalt_method",        "soil_co_depth$"     = "soil_cobalt_depth"
)

#' Try to turn an unrecognized column name into a known official field name
#' by applying common naming-variant fixes. Returns NA if no fix applies.
suggest_column_name <- function(colname) {
  s <- tolower(trimws(colname))
  s <- gsub(" ", "_", s)
  s <- gsub("__", "_", s)

  for (i in seq_along(COLUMN_NAME_ALIASES)) {
    s <- gsub(names(COLUMN_NAME_ALIASES)[i], COLUMN_NAME_ALIASES[i], s, ignore.case = TRUE)
  }
  for (i in seq_along(SOIL_NAME_ALIASES)) {
    s <- gsub(names(SOIL_NAME_ALIASES)[i], SOIL_NAME_ALIASES[i], s, ignore.case = TRUE)
  }
  for (i in seq_along(TAXA_NAME_FIXES)) {
    s <- gsub(names(TAXA_NAME_FIXES)[i], TAXA_NAME_FIXES[i], s, ignore.case = TRUE)
  }

  if (tolower(s) == tolower(colname)) return(NA_character_)
  s
}

# Transect-level metadata fields: these describe the transect/plot as a
# whole and should therefore be constant across all rows sharing the same
# transect_ID. Monolith-level fields (monolith_management,
# monolith_microhabitat...) are intentionally excluded since they are
# expected to vary within a transect. sample_year is included as a
# manual-review flag: it may legitimately vary for genuinely repeated /
# temporal datasets, in which case the warning can simply be dismissed.
CONSTANT_WITHIN_TRANSECT_VARS <- c(
  "locality", "sample_dim", "extraction_method",
  "lu_cover", "management", "dominant_plant_species", "trees",
  "native_LU", "former_lu", "time_since_former_LU", "lu_history",
  "agroforestry", "horticulture", "orchard", "annual_crop",
  "integrated_system", "integrated_system_details",
  "irrigated", "irrigation_details", "rotation", "rotation_details",
  "crop_residue_management", "cover_crop", "cover_crop_details",
  "monoculture_polyculture", "biomass_management",
  "tillage", "tillage_time", "tillage_depth", "tillage_details",
  "grazing", "grazing_details", "mowing", "sowing", "mechanical_force",
  "pesticides", "pesticides_details", "herbicide", "fungicide",
  "insecticide", "nematicide",
  "fertilizers", "fertilizers_organic", "fertilizers_organic_details",
  "fertilizers_chemical", "fertilizers_chemical_details",
  "metal_pollution", "urban", "chronosequence", "fire_management",
  "veg_height", "sample_year"
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Generic sheet-finding helpers
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#' Find the first sheet in `path` whose name matches one of `patterns`
#' (tried in order), optionally excluding some sheet names.
find_sheet <- function(path, patterns, exclude = character(0)) {
  sheets <- tryCatch(readxl::excel_sheets(path), error = function(e) character(0))
  if (length(sheets) == 0) return(NA_character_)

  sheets_low  <- tolower(trimws(sheets))
  exclude_low <- tolower(trimws(exclude))

  for (p in patterns) {
    idx <- which(grepl(p, sheets_low, ignore.case = TRUE) & !(sheets_low %in% exclude_low))
    if (length(idx) > 0) return(sheets[idx[1]])
  }
  NA_character_
}

#' Auto-detect the header row of the dataset sheet by scanning the first
#' `max_scan` rows for known column names (works regardless of how many
#' comment/example rows precede the header, so it is robust across template
#' versions). Falls back to row 7 (the current template's convention: 6
#' rows of comments/examples + header) if detection fails.
detect_header_row <- function(path, sheet, max_scan = 20,
                               anchor_terms = c("datasetid", "data_provider", "contact_mail",
                                                 "transect_id", "monolith_id",
                                                 "latitude", "longitude", "sample_year",
                                                 "plot_id", "country")) {
  raw <- tryCatch(
    readxl::read_excel(path, sheet = sheet, col_names = FALSE, n_max = max_scan),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  if (is.null(raw) || nrow(raw) == 0) {
    return(list(row = 7L, detected = FALSE))
  }

  norm <- function(x) tolower(trimws(gsub("[^a-zA-Z0-9_]", "", as.character(x))))
  scores <- apply(raw, 1, function(r) sum(anchor_terms %in% norm(r)))

  best <- which.max(scores)
  if (length(best) == 0 || scores[best] < 2) {
    return(list(row = min(7L, nrow(raw)), detected = FALSE))
  }
  list(row = as.integer(best), detected = TRUE)
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Reading the dataset sheet
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#' Read the "dataset" sheet of an uploaded workbook, auto-detecting both the
#' sheet name and the header row. Returns a list with the sheet used, the
#' header row number, whether detection was confident, the raw (unmodified)
#' header row (to catch true duplicate column names before readxl renames
#' them), and the imported data.
read_dataset_sheet <- function(path) {
  sheet <- find_sheet(path, DATASET_SHEET_PATTERNS, exclude = METADATA_EXCLUDE_FROM_DATASET)
  if (is.na(sheet)) {
    stop("Could not find a 'dataset' sheet. Sheets found in the file: ",
         paste(tryCatch(readxl::excel_sheets(path), error = function(e) "?"), collapse = ", "))
  }

  hd <- detect_header_row(path, sheet)
  header_row <- hd$row

  raw_header <- tryCatch(
    readxl::read_excel(path, sheet = sheet, col_names = FALSE,
                        skip = header_row - 1, n_max = 1) |>
      unlist(use.names = FALSE) |> as.character(),
    error = function(e) character(0)
  )

  df <- readxl::read_excel(
    path, sheet = sheet,
    skip = header_row - 1,
    na = NA_CODES,
    guess_max = 5000
  )

  list(
    sheet = sheet,
    header_row = header_row,
    header_detected = hd$detected,
    raw_header = raw_header,
    data = df
  )
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Building the validation spec from the official template
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#' Parse the "Variables explanation" sheet of the official template into a
#' tidy spec table: field, mandatory_type ("hard" / "soft" / "none"),
#' value_or_unit, example, description.
build_field_spec <- function(path) {
  sheet <- find_sheet(path, SPEC_SHEET_PATTERNS)
  if (is.na(sheet)) return(tibble::tibble())

  raw <- tryCatch(
    readxl::read_excel(path, sheet = sheet, col_names = FALSE),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  if (is.null(raw) || nrow(raw) == 0) return(tibble::tibble())

  header_idx <- which(tolower(trimws(as.character(raw[[1]]))) == "fields")[1]
  if (is.na(header_idx) || header_idx >= nrow(raw)) return(tibble::tibble())

  body <- raw[(header_idx + 1):nrow(raw), 1:6]
  names(body) <- c("field", "mandatory", "value_or_unit", "col4", "example", "description")

  body <- body |>
    dplyr::mutate(field = trimws(as.character(field))) |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(field), field != "")

  # Real field names never contain whitespace (they are snake_case). The
  # sheet ends with free-text notes ("Notes regarding taxa abundances",
  # "The different types of data...") which DO contain spaces: use that as
  # a robust stop condition instead of hardcoding a row number.
  stop_idx <- which(grepl("\\s", body$field))[1]
  if (!is.na(stop_idx)) {
    body <- if (stop_idx > 1) body[1:(stop_idx - 1), ] else body[0, ]
  }

  body |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      mandatory_raw  = trimws(as.character(mandatory)),
      mandatory_type = dplyr::case_when(
        is.na(mandatory_raw) | mandatory_raw == "" ~ "none",
        tolower(mandatory_raw) == "yes"             ~ "hard",
        TRUE                                        ~ "soft"
      ),
      value_or_unit = trimws(as.character(value_or_unit)),
      example       = trimws(as.character(example)),
      description   = trimws(as.character(description))
    ) |>
    dplyr::select(field, mandatory_type, mandatory_raw, value_or_unit, example, description)
}

#' Extract a controlled vocabulary (allowed values) from a "value or unit"
#' spec string, e.g. "yes/no" -> c("yes","no"), or
#' '"open" or "restricted"' -> c("open","restricted"). Returns NULL if no
#' controlled vocabulary is detected.
extract_allowed_values <- function(spec_str) {
  if (is.na(spec_str) || trimws(spec_str) == "") return(NULL)

  quoted <- regmatches(spec_str, gregexpr('"[^"]+"', spec_str))[[1]]
  if (length(quoted) >= 2) {
    return(tolower(gsub('"', "", quoted)))
  }

  s <- tolower(spec_str)
  if (grepl("^[a-z_ ]+(/[a-z_ ]+)+$", s)) {
    return(trimws(strsplit(s, "/")[[1]]))
  }

  NULL
}

#' Classify a "value or unit" spec string into a check type used by
#' validate_dataset().
classify_check_type <- function(value_or_unit) {
  s <- tolower(trimws(value_or_unit))
  if (is.na(s) || s == "") return("text")

  if (s == "email")                                 return("email")
  if (grepl("^html link", s))                        return("url")
  if (s == "yyyy")                                    return("year")
  if (s == "mm")                                       return("month")
  if (s == "dd")                                        return("day")
  if (s == "tt")                                         return("hour")
  if (grepl("decimal degree", s))                        return("decimal")
  if (grepl("start by a letter", s))                     return("start_letter")
  if (grepl("lower case", s))                            return("lowercase")
  if (!is.null(extract_allowed_values(value_or_unit)))   return("enum")
  if (grepl("^number$", s) ||
      grepl("meter|years|percent|%|g_kg|cmolc_kg|mg_g|ds_m|g_g|mg_m3|^m$|^cm$", s)) return("numeric")

  "text"
}

# Build the spec once at app startup from the bundled reference template.
# If anything goes wrong (missing/changed file), the app keeps working with
# an empty spec: spec-based checks are simply skipped (see validate_dataset).
FIELD_SPEC <- tryCatch(
  build_field_spec(REFERENCE_TEMPLATE_PATH),
  error = function(e) tibble::tibble()
)

# Human-readable diagnostic of what the app actually found at startup, shown
# in the Issues tab whenever FIELD_SPEC is empty, so the problem can be
# self-diagnosed (wrong working directory at runtime, folder not deployed,
# no .xlsx in it, etc.) instead of just saying "could not be loaded".
FIELD_SPEC_DIAGNOSTIC <- if (nrow(FIELD_SPEC) > 0) {
  sprintf("Reference template loaded OK: %s (%d fields parsed).", REFERENCE_TEMPLATE_PATH, nrow(FIELD_SPEC))
} else {
  dir_exists <- dir.exists(REFERENCE_TEMPLATE_DIR)
  files_in_dir <- if (dir_exists) list.files(REFERENCE_TEMPLATE_DIR) else character(0)
  sheet_found <- if (!is.na(REFERENCE_TEMPLATE_PATH)) {
    find_sheet(REFERENCE_TEMPLATE_PATH, SPEC_SHEET_PATTERNS)
  } else {
    NA_character_
  }

  paste(
    "Reference spec could not be built. Diagnostic:",
    paste0("- Working directory at startup: ", getwd()),
    paste0("- Looking for folder: '", REFERENCE_TEMPLATE_DIR, "' -> exists: ", dir_exists),
    paste0("- Files found in that folder: ", if (length(files_in_dir) > 0) paste(files_in_dir, collapse = ", ") else "(none / folder not found)"),
    paste0("- .xlsx file selected: ", ifelse(is.na(REFERENCE_TEMPLATE_PATH), "(none found)", REFERENCE_TEMPLATE_PATH)),
    paste0("- 'Variables explanation' sheet found in it: ", ifelse(is.na(sheet_found), "NO", sheet_found)),
    sep = "\n"
  )
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Generic validators
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#' Count rows and distinct sampling units (transects / monoliths / layers)
#' present in the dataset, when the corresponding ID columns exist.
count_structure <- function(df) {
  n_distinct_safe <- function(x) {
    if (is.null(x)) return(NA_integer_)
    length(unique(x[!is.na(x)]))
  }
  list(
    n_rows      = nrow(df),
    n_transects = n_distinct_safe(df[["transect_ID"]]),
    n_monoliths = n_distinct_safe(df[["monolith_ID"]]),
    n_layers    = n_distinct_safe(df[["layer_ID"]])
  )
}

is_open_prefixed <- function(x) {
  any(vapply(OPEN_PREFIXES, function(p) grepl(p, x), logical(1)))
}

#' TRUE = value is numeric or missing, FALSE = value present but not numeric.
check_numeric_vec <- function(x) {
  if (is.numeric(x)) return(rep(TRUE, length(x)))
  suppressWarnings(!is.na(as.numeric(as.character(x))) | is.na(x))
}

#' Run the full validation suite on an imported dataset (as returned by
#' read_dataset_sheet()) against the field spec (as returned by
#' build_field_spec()). Returns a list with a tidy `issues` table and
#' error/warning/info counts.
validate_dataset <- function(imported, spec) {
  df         <- imported$data
  raw_header <- imported$raw_header
  cols       <- colnames(df)

  issues <- list()
  add_issue <- function(column, severity, message, n_affected = NA_integer_, examples = NA_character_) {
    issues[[length(issues) + 1]] <<- tibble::tibble(
      column = column, severity = severity, message = message,
      n_affected = n_affected, examples = examples
    )
  }

  ## 1. Duplicated column names in the ORIGINAL header (before readxl renames them)
  hdr_clean <- trimws(as.character(raw_header))
  hdr_clean <- hdr_clean[!is.na(hdr_clean) & hdr_clean != ""]
  dups <- unique(hdr_clean[duplicated(tolower(hdr_clean))])
  if (length(dups) > 0) {
    add_issue(paste(dups, collapse = ", "), "error",
               "Duplicated column name(s) found in the original file header.")
  }

  ## 1b. Empty / unnamed column headers in the ORIGINAL file (shows up as
  ## "...N" once readxl auto-names them)
  empty_hdr_pos <- which(is.na(raw_header) | trimws(as.character(raw_header)) == "")
  empty_hdr_pos <- empty_hdr_pos[empty_hdr_pos <= length(cols)]
  if (length(empty_hdr_pos) > 0) {
    add_issue(paste(cols[empty_hdr_pos], collapse = ", "), "warning",
               sprintf("Empty/unnamed column header(s) in the original file at position(s): %s.",
                       paste(empty_hdr_pos, collapse = ", ")))
  }

  ## 1c. Abundance columns ending in _se / _sd look like aggregated summary
  ## statistics (standard error / standard deviation) rather than raw
  ## per-sample values, which is not the expected format.
  se_sd_cols <- cols[grepl("^(d_|fbm_|dbm_).*(_se|_sd)$", cols, ignore.case = TRUE)]
  if (length(se_sd_cols) > 0) {
    add_issue(paste(se_sd_cols, collapse = ", "), "warning",
               "Column(s) look like aggregated statistics (_se/_sd suffix) rather than raw per-sample abundance values.")
  }

  ## 2. Missing mandatory columns
  if (nrow(spec) > 0) {
    hard_fields <- spec$field[spec$mandatory_type == "hard"]
    missing <- setdiff(hard_fields, cols)
    missing <- missing[!vapply(missing, is_open_prefixed, logical(1))]
    if (length(missing) > 0) {
      add_issue(paste(missing, collapse = ", "), "error",
                 "Mandatory column(s) missing from the dataset sheet.")
    }
  }

  ## 3. Empty columns (100% NA)
  empty_cols <- cols[vapply(df, function(x) all(is.na(x)), logical(1))]
  if (length(empty_cols) > 0) {
    add_issue(paste(empty_cols, collapse = ", "), "info",
               "Column(s) entirely empty (all NA) - consider removing them.")
  }

  ## 4. Legacy taxon-column names are accepted, but the current prefix-based
  ## convention is recommended. They are reported separately and are not
  ## subsequently classified as unknown columns.
  legacy_taxa_cols <- cols[is_legacy_taxa_column(cols)]
  if (length(legacy_taxa_cols) > 0) {
    for (fld in legacy_taxa_cols) {
      suggested <- current_taxa_column_name(fld)
      add_issue(
        fld,
        "warning",
        sprintf(
          "Legacy taxon column name accepted. Consider renaming '%s' to '%s' to follow the current GSMF convention.",
          fld, suggested
        ),
        NA_integer_,
        suggested
      )
    }
  }

  ## 4b. Unrecognized columns (not in spec, not an open-ended prefix family)
  if (nrow(spec) > 0) {
    unknown <- setdiff(cols, c(spec$field, ALLOWED_EXTRA_COLUMNS, legacy_taxa_cols))
    unknown <- unknown[!vapply(unknown, is_open_prefixed, logical(1))]

    if (length(unknown) > 0) {
      spec_fields_lower <- tolower(spec$field)
      no_suggestion <- character(0)

      for (u in unknown) {
        candidate <- suggest_column_name(u)
        match_idx <- if (!is.na(candidate)) which(spec_fields_lower == tolower(candidate)) else integer(0)

        if (length(match_idx) > 0) {
          add_issue(u, "warning",
                     sprintf("Column not found in the official template - looks like a naming variant of '%s'. Consider renaming it.",
                             spec$field[match_idx[1]]))
        } else {
          no_suggestion <- c(no_suggestion, u)
        }
      }

      if (length(no_suggestion) > 0) {
        add_issue(paste(no_suggestion, collapse = ", "), "warning",
                   "Column name(s) not found in the official template spec (check spelling / naming convention).")
      }
    }
  }

  ## 5. Per-column controlled-vocabulary / type checks (from the spec)
  if (nrow(spec) > 0) {
    for (i in seq_len(nrow(spec))) {
      fld <- spec$field[i]
      if (!(fld %in% cols)) next
      x <- df[[fld]]
      vtype <- if (
        fld %in% TEXT_FIELDS ||
        grepl(UNIT_COLUMN_PATTERN, fld, ignore.case = TRUE)
      ) {
        "text"
      } else {
        classify_check_type(spec$value_or_unit[i])
      }
      if (vtype == "text") next

      valid <- switch(vtype,
        "enum" = {
          allowed <- extract_allowed_values(spec$value_or_unit[i])
          tolower(trimws(as.character(x))) %in% allowed | is.na(x)
        },
        # Kept deliberately lenient (just requires an "@") rather than a
        # strict format regex: the strict version kept flagging visibly
        # valid addresses (likely invisible/non-ASCII characters introduced
        # by copy-pasting into Excel), causing more noise than value.
        "email"        = grepl("@", as.character(x), fixed = TRUE) | is.na(x),
        "url"          = grepl("^https?://", trimws(as.character(x)), ignore.case = TRUE) | is.na(x),
        "year"         = { yr <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(x)));
                            is.na(x) | (!is.na(yr) & yr >= 1900 & yr <= as.numeric(format(Sys.Date(), "%Y"))) },
        "month"        = { m <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(x)));
                            is.na(x) | (!is.na(m) & m >= 1 & m <= 12) },
        "day"          = { d <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(x)));
                            is.na(x) | (!is.na(d) & d >= 1 & d <= 31) },
        "hour"         = { h <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(x)));
                            is.na(x) | (!is.na(h) & h >= 0 & h <= 24) },
        "decimal"      = check_numeric_vec(x),
        "numeric"      = check_numeric_vec(x),
        "start_letter" = grepl("^[a-zA-Z]", trimws(as.character(x))) | is.na(x),
        "lowercase"    = { v <- as.character(x); is.na(x) | v == tolower(v) },
        rep(TRUE, length(x))
      )

      n_bad <- sum(!valid, na.rm = TRUE)
      if (n_bad > 0) {
        bad_vals <- unique(as.character(x[!valid]))
        ex  <- paste(utils::head(bad_vals, 5), collapse = " | ")
        # NB: "numeric"/"decimal" checks are kept as warnings rather than
        # errors, because a few fields in the official spec sheet are
        # mislabeled as "number" while actually expecting text (e.g.
        # spatial_setting, sample_shape) - flagging them as hard errors
        # would create false positives on otherwise valid data.
        sev <- if (vtype %in% c("enum", "email", "year", "month", "day")) "error" else "warning"
        add_issue(fld, sev,
                   sprintf("%d value(s) do not match the expected format (expected: %s).", n_bad, spec$value_or_unit[i]),
                   n_bad, ex)
      }
    }
  }

  ## 6. Geographic range checks
  if ("latitude" %in% cols) {
    lat <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(df$latitude)))
    bad <- !is.na(lat) & (lat < -90 | lat > 90)
    if (any(bad)) add_issue("latitude", "error", "Latitude out of range [-90, 90].", sum(bad))
  }
  if ("longitude" %in% cols) {
    lon <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(df$longitude)))
    bad <- !is.na(lon) & (lon < -180 | lon > 180)
    if (any(bad)) add_issue("longitude", "error", "Longitude out of range [-180, 180].", sum(bad))
  }

  ## 7. Abundance columns in both current and legacy naming conventions must
  ## be numeric and non-negative.
  abun_cols <- cols[is_taxa_column(cols)]
  for (fld in abun_cols) {
    valid <- check_numeric_vec(df[[fld]])
    n_bad <- sum(!valid, na.rm = TRUE)
    if (n_bad > 0) {
      add_issue(fld, "error", sprintf("%d non-numeric value(s) in this abundance column.", n_bad), n_bad)
    }

    x_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(df[[fld]])))
    n_neg <- sum(x_num < 0, na.rm = TRUE)
    if (n_neg > 0) {
      add_issue(fld, "error", sprintf("%d negative value(s) found in this abundance column (should be >= 0).", n_neg), n_neg)
    }
  }

  ## 7b. Column names containing a '.' - typically a sign of duplicated or
  ## misaligned columns from the original Excel file.
  dot_cols <- cols[grepl("\\.", cols)]
  if (length(dot_cols) > 0) {
    add_issue(paste(dot_cols, collapse = ", "), "warning",
               "Column name(s) contain a '.' - often a sign of duplicated/misaligned columns in the original file.")
  }

  ## 8. Fully-empty rows
  empty_rows <- sum(rowSums(!is.na(df)) == 0)
  if (empty_rows > 0) {
    add_issue("(rows)", "info", sprintf("%d row(s) are entirely empty.", empty_rows))
  }

  ## 9. Fields that MUST be filled on every single row for the dataset to be
  ## acceptable: transect ID, coordinates, date, and land-use description.
  required_row_fields <- c("transect_ID", "latitude", "longitude", "sample_year", "sample_month",
                            "lu_cover", "management")
  for (fld in required_row_fields) {
    if (fld %in% cols) {
      n_missing <- sum(is.na(df[[fld]]))
      if (n_missing > 0) {
        add_issue(fld, "error",
                   sprintf("%d row(s) missing a value for this field - it must be filled on every row.", n_missing),
                   n_missing)
      }
    }
  }

  ## 10. Land-use / management metadata should be constant within a transect
  ## (transect-level info should not vary row-to-row for the same transect_ID)
  if ("transect_ID" %in% cols) {
    constant_vars <- intersect(CONSTANT_WITHIN_TRANSECT_VARS, cols)
    if (length(constant_vars) > 0) {
      incons <- df |>
        dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(c("transect_ID", constant_vars))) |>
        dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data[["transect_ID"]])) |>
        dplyr::group_by(.data[["transect_ID"]]) |>
        dplyr::summarise(
          dplyr::across(dplyr::all_of(constant_vars), ~ length(unique(.x[!is.na(.x)]))),
          .groups = "drop"
        ) |>
        tidyr::pivot_longer(-transect_ID, names_to = "variable", values_to = "n_distinct") |>
        dplyr::filter(n_distinct > 1)

      if (nrow(incons) > 0) {
        by_var <- incons |> dplyr::count(variable, name = "n_transects_affected")
        for (i in seq_len(nrow(by_var))) {
          add_issue(by_var$variable[i], "warning",
                     sprintf("Value varies within %d transect(s) sharing the same transect_ID (usually expected to be constant at transect level).",
                             by_var$n_transects_affected[i]),
                     by_var$n_transects_affected[i])
        }
      }
    }
  }

  issues_df <- if (length(issues) == 0) {
    tibble::tibble(column = character(), severity = character(),
                    message = character(), n_affected = integer(), examples = character())
  } else {
    dplyr::bind_rows(issues)
  }

  list(
    issues     = issues_df,
    n_errors   = sum(issues_df$severity == "error"),
    n_warnings = sum(issues_df$severity == "warning"),
    n_info     = sum(issues_df$severity == "info")
  )
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Metadata sheet: best-effort key/value extraction
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#' The "metadata to fill" sheet is a free-form questionnaire (labels and
#' answers scattered in merged cells), not a table, so it cannot be parsed
#' reliably in a fully generic way. This function does a best-effort search
#' for a handful of key labels and returns the nearest non-empty cell as the
#' likely answer, PLUS the raw sheet so the user can always double-check
#' manually.
extract_metadata_summary <- function(path) {
  sheet <- find_sheet(path, METADATA_SHEET_PATTERNS)

  keys <- list(
    "Dataset ID"              = "dataset_?id",
    "Data provider"           = "^name$",
    "Publication reference / DOI" = "doi|publication reference",
    "Sampling goal"           = "sampling goal|purpose of the study"
  )

  if (is.na(sheet)) {
    empty_table <- tibble::tibble(field = names(keys), value = NA_character_)
    return(list(sheet = NA_character_, table = empty_table, raw = tibble::tibble()))
  }

  raw <- tryCatch(
    readxl::read_excel(path, sheet = sheet, col_names = FALSE),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  if (is.null(raw) || nrow(raw) == 0) {
    empty_table <- tibble::tibble(field = names(keys), value = NA_character_)
    return(list(sheet = sheet, table = empty_table, raw = tibble::tibble()))
  }

  raw_chr <- as.data.frame(lapply(raw, as.character), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

  find_value_near <- function(r, c) {
    nr <- nrow(raw_chr); ncc <- ncol(raw_chr)

    if (c < ncc) {
      for (cc in (c + 1):min(c + 8, ncc)) {
        v <- raw_chr[r, cc]
        if (!is.na(v) && trimws(v) != "") return(trimws(v))
      }
    }
    if (r < nr) {
      for (rr in (r + 1):min(r + 2, nr)) {
        for (cc in max(1, c - 1):min(c + 8, ncc)) {
          v <- raw_chr[rr, cc]
          if (!is.na(v) && trimws(v) != "") return(trimws(v))
        }
      }
    }
    NA_character_
  }

  rows_out <- vector("list", length(keys))
  for (i in seq_along(keys)) {
    label   <- names(keys)[i]
    pattern <- keys[[i]]
    hit <- NULL
    for (r in seq_len(nrow(raw_chr))) {
      for (c in seq_len(ncol(raw_chr))) {
        v <- raw_chr[r, c]
        if (!is.na(v) && grepl(pattern, v, ignore.case = TRUE)) { hit <- c(r, c); break }
      }
      if (!is.null(hit)) break
    }
    val <- if (is.null(hit)) NA_character_ else find_value_near(hit[1], hit[2])
    rows_out[[i]] <- tibble::tibble(field = label, value = val)
  }

  list(sheet = sheet, table = dplyr::bind_rows(rows_out), raw = raw_chr)
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Hierarchical ID integrity checks
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#' Check that sampling-unit identifiers respect the GSMF hierarchy.
#'
#' Expected relationships:
#'   plot_ID -> transect_ID -> monolith_ID -> layer_ID
#'
#' A parent may contain several children, but a child identifier must never
#' occur under more than one parent identifier. For example, the same
#' monolith_ID cannot be used in two different transect_ID values.
#'
#' The function also reports rows where a child ID is filled but its immediate
#' parent ID is missing. Missing child IDs are not flagged because transect-
#' level and monolith-level records may legitimately have no lower-level ID.
check_id_integrity <- function(df) {

  empty_result <- function() {
    tibble::tibble(
      check = character(),
      severity = character(),
      child_column = character(),
      child_id = character(),
      parent_column = character(),
      parent_values = character(),
      n_rows = integer(),
      message = character()
    )
  }

  if (is.null(df) || nrow(df) == 0) {
    return(list(
      issues = empty_result(),
      n_errors = 0L,
      n_warnings = 0L,
      n_checks_run = 0L
    ))
  }

  # Resolve names case-insensitively while preserving the names actually used
  # in the uploaded workbook.
  resolve_col <- function(target) {
    hit <- which(tolower(names(df)) == tolower(target))
    if (length(hit) == 0) return(NA_character_)
    names(df)[hit[1]]
  }

  hierarchy <- tibble::tribble(
    ~parent_target, ~child_target,
    "plot_ID",     "transect_ID",
    "transect_ID", "monolith_ID",
    "monolith_ID", "layer_ID"
  )

  out <- list()
  n_checks_run <- 0L

  clean_id <- function(x) {
    x <- trimws(as.character(x))
    x[is.na(x) | x == ""] <- NA_character_
    x
  }

  for (i in seq_len(nrow(hierarchy))) {
    parent_col <- resolve_col(hierarchy$parent_target[i])
    child_col  <- resolve_col(hierarchy$child_target[i])

    # A relationship can only be checked when both columns exist.
    if (is.na(parent_col) || is.na(child_col)) next
    n_checks_run <- n_checks_run + 1L

    rel <- tibble::tibble(
      parent = clean_id(df[[parent_col]]),
      child  = clean_id(df[[child_col]])
    )

    # 1. One child ID associated with several parent IDs.
    conflicts <- rel |>
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(child), !is.na(parent)) |>
      dplyr::distinct(child, parent) |>
      dplyr::group_by(child) |>
      dplyr::summarise(
        n_parents = dplyr::n_distinct(parent),
        parent_values = paste(sort(unique(parent)), collapse = " | "),
        .groups = "drop"
      ) |>
      dplyr::filter(n_parents > 1)

    if (nrow(conflicts) > 0) {
      row_counts <- rel |>
        dplyr::filter(!is.na(child)) |>
        dplyr::count(child, name = "n_rows")

      conflicts <- conflicts |>
        dplyr::left_join(row_counts, by = "child")

      for (j in seq_len(nrow(conflicts))) {
        out[[length(out) + 1L]] <- tibble::tibble(
          check = paste0(child_col, " -> ", parent_col),
          severity = "error",
          child_column = child_col,
          child_id = conflicts$child[j],
          parent_column = parent_col,
          parent_values = conflicts$parent_values[j],
          n_rows = as.integer(conflicts$n_rows[j]),
          message = sprintf(
            "%s '%s' is associated with %d different %s values.",
            child_col,
            conflicts$child[j],
            conflicts$n_parents[j],
            parent_col
          )
        )
      }
    }

    # 2. Child ID present while its immediate parent ID is missing.
    missing_parent <- rel |>
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(child), is.na(parent)) |>
      dplyr::count(child, name = "n_rows")

    if (nrow(missing_parent) > 0) {
      for (j in seq_len(nrow(missing_parent))) {
        out[[length(out) + 1L]] <- tibble::tibble(
          check = paste0(child_col, " -> ", parent_col),
          severity = "warning",
          child_column = child_col,
          child_id = missing_parent$child[j],
          parent_column = parent_col,
          parent_values = NA_character_,
          n_rows = as.integer(missing_parent$n_rows[j]),
          message = sprintf(
            "%s '%s' is present on %d row(s), but %s is missing.",
            child_col,
            missing_parent$child[j],
            missing_parent$n_rows[j],
            parent_col
          )
        )
      }
    }
  }

  issues <- if (length(out) == 0) empty_result() else dplyr::bind_rows(out)

  list(
    issues = issues,
    n_errors = sum(issues$severity == "error"),
    n_warnings = sum(issues$severity == "warning"),
    n_checks_run = n_checks_run
  )
}
